title: Analyzer conformance fixture manifest
description: Language-neutral contract for one captured (or synthetic) analyzer message used as a conformance fixture. The simulator harness and any future driver consume the same fixtures; the manifest carries provenance for ISO 15189 traceability.

id.description: Stable, kebab-case fixture id, unique within the fixtures tree.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this message exercises.

protocol.description: Application protocol of the captured message.

transport.description: Transport the real instrument speaks; the harness replays via a matching Transport or protocol-specific session simulator.

path.description: Raw application-payload bytes file, relative to this manifest. Wire framing is NOT stored here — the transport applies it at replay time.

framing.description: Wire framing the transport must apply/strip around the payload.

reference.description: Provenance: instrument manual section, bench-capture session id, or synthetic-seed note (ISO 15189 evidence chain).

synthetic.description: true = not a real instrument capture (illustrative/skeleton seed). Real conformance captures set false.

channel.description: Per-analyzer channel configuration data. This captures the physical/listener settings required to run the fixture without baking them into parser code.

isolation_group.description: Independent channel/driver group this analyzer belongs to; faults in one group must not require parser or core changes for another.

provenance.description: Where the line-setting values came from. 'bridge-default-bench-pending' marks unconfirmed defaults that must be replaced by a bench capture or vendor doc before go-live.

tcp.description: Dedicated TCP listener settings for analyzers attached via a per-analyzer TCP/ASTM channel rather than shared serial (LIS-175).

provenance.description: Where the listener settings came from. 'bridge-default-bench-pending' marks unconfirmed defaults that must be replaced by a bench capture or vendor doc before go-live.

identity.description: Analyzer-side Online-screen identity settings and the cross-reference to the bridge's canonical analyzer-channel registry entry (LIS-175).

provenance.description: Where the identity settings came from. 'bridge-default-bench-pending' marks unconfirmed defaults that must be replaced by a bench capture or vendor doc before go-live.

bridge_registry_id.description: Cross-reference to this channel's canonical id in the bridge's bridge.analyzers registry.

terminology.description: Per-analyzer vendor-code and unit mapping data used by the simulator/bridge analogs.

note.description: Provenance/caveat for this mapping table — e.g. a synthetic seed disclaiming complete or site-verified assay coverage pending a real capture (ISO 15189 traceability).

expected.description: Placeholder for downstream parser/normalization assertions (e.g. the normalized LOINC/UCUM Result row). Wired up once those services land in Stage 1.

capture.description: Bench-capture provenance for a graduated (synthetic: false) fixture: who/what/when produced the raw artifact this manifest is derived from, and whether that artifact is verifiable in-repo (ISO 15189 evidence chain). Loader-enforced coupling: required whenever synthetic is false.

source_kind.description: 'bench-capture' = verbatim (redacted) real bytes with the raw artifact stored in-repo, digest-verified on every load. 'bench-derived' = a structurally re-synthesized artifact whose pristine original lives ONLY in the offline evidence store -- CI cannot verify its digest. 'bench-recombined' = records drawn verbatim from the cited in-repo raw capture but re-framed/re-sequenced (e.g. multi-envelope flattened to one transmission); the record-level containment relationship is loader-verified; raw_path is required and digest-verified like 'bench-capture'.

raw_digest.description: sha256 of the raw capture artifact's bytes, 'sha256:<hex>'. Verified against capture.raw_path on every load for source_kind 'bench-capture'; not verifiable in CI for 'bench-derived' (the pristine original is offline-only).

raw_path.description: Repo-root-relative path to the raw capture artifact. Required for source_kind 'bench-capture' and 'bench-recombined'; forbidden for 'bench-derived' -- enforced by the loader.

firmware.description: Firmware version string; null = NOT RECORDED at capture time.

chassis_connected.description: false = the analyzer chassis was disconnected; every 2026-07-17 capture is a stored-result replay.

unconfirmed_channel_settings.description: Names of channel sub-blocks (e.g. "tcp", "rs232") whose provenance is NOT "bench-capture". A graduated fixture must declare every such sub-block here explicitly; completeness is loader-enforced.

spec.description: Path to the derivation spec document.

ledger.description: Path to the sanitization.json transformation ledger.

derivation.description: Required for source_kind 'bench-derived'; forbidden for 'bench-capture' -- enforced by the loader.

normalized_fields.description: Maps an ASTM record-type letter to the 1-based field indices that the recombination legitimately rewrote (e.g. {"O": [2]}). Field 1 is always the record-type letter itself and must never be declared here -- enforced by the loader. Note: this repo's hand-rolled schema validator (edge_sim/_schema.py) does not propagate constraints through an open-ended "additionalProperties" map, so the record-type keys are enumerated explicitly (the ASTM E1394 record types) rather than left as an arbitrary-key map, to keep the array/integer typing here actually schema-enforced.

note.description: Human-readable account of the recombination transformation.

recombination.description: Required for source_kind 'bench-recombined'; forbidden otherwise -- enforced by the loader.
